mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal zero lower bound monetary accommodation. This extreme easing compresses equity risk premiums and reduces discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.05% on 2020-04-01 indicates ZLB regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates sharply (PAYEMS decline > 2% annualized rate). This signals demand destruction and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS fell from 131045 to 130426 thousand persons in April 2020, a -0.47% decline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite sharp payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals consumer demand destruction. Combined with housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million annualized units, this dual demand weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates contract.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND HOUST < 1.2 million annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 403946M and HOUST at 1216K on 2020-03-01 indicate demand stress
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumer and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST trigger conditions are met, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 90 index points signal severe supply-side contraction. This indicates capacity utilization collapse and forward earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.59 vintage (84.56 revised) on 2020-04-01 indicates sharp supply contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 90, or Rank IC between INDPRO and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 10% signals severe labor market slack and demand destruction. This regime shift triggers equity repricing as forward earnings estimates fall and risk premiums expand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 14.7% on 2020-04-01 indicates severe unemployment spike
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when UNRATE > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains near zero. This deflationary signal combined with monetary accommodation indicates real rate compression and equity valuation support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 255.90 and FEDFUNDS at 0.05% on 2020-04-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both deflation and ZLB conditions are met, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates to below 1% annualized rate (measured quarter-over-quarter). This signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings multiples compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage at 18987.88 with revised value 20709.21 on 2020-01-01 indicates measurement uncertainty in crisis period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) contraction signals synchronized supply and labor destruction. This dual shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity markets immediately reprice earnings and growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO fell from 93.77 to 92.59 and PAYEMS fell from 131045 to 130426 in April 2020
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline triggers are met, or the interaction term (INDPRO × PAYEMS) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.
